text.title: python-pytest-fixtures Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for python-pytest-fixtures. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use the smallest fixture scope that keeps test execution time acceptable. Prefer function scope unless a measured performance cost demands widening. A function-scope fixture that takes 50 ms is acceptable; one that takes 3 seconds per test is a candidate for module or session scope.
rule.rationale: Wider scopes share state between tests. If any test mutates shared state and another test relies on a clean state, a random execution order (pytest-randomly) will expose this as a flake. Function scope is the safe default.

rule.statement: A wider-scope fixture MUST NOT depend on a narrower-scope fixture. A session-scoped fixture cannot take a function-scoped fixture as a parameter — pytest raises a ScopeMismatch error at collection time.
rule.rationale: A session fixture lives longer than a function fixture; requesting the shorter-lived resource in the longer-lived context is a lifecycle contract violation.
